description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking red fuselage and dual wings, each adorned with black crosses outlined in white, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane features a single open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing, with a visible propeller mounted at the nose. The wings are supported by a network of struts and wires, providing structural integrity and a classic biplane silhouette. The tail section includes a rounded vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both painted red and supported by additional bracing wires. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The surface of the airplane shows signs of weathering and wear, adding to its historical character.
description: A red vintage biplane with dual wings, black crosses, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, showing signs of wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, central propeller, and open cockpit behind the engine.
description_view_1: Angled top-left view highlighting the upper wing's markings, cockpit placement, and strut structure.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, tail design, and symmetrical wing markings.
description_view_3: Angled top-right view emphasizing the propeller, wing struts, and tail assembly.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, supported by bracing wires.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with no canopy.
